components.title: 1.1 Innovation strategy

outputs.title: Research and innovation strategy defined for connected digital twins in transport
activities: Understand the landscape of activities from those engaged in research and innovation relevant to this roadmap, plus key industry stakeholders and consumers of digital twins
Carry out digital twin maturity assessment
Formulate a connected digital twins research and innovation strategy co-created by TRIB members, including government, funding bodies, and industry
Establish forums and frameworks for collaboration on research and development
Identify core opportunities that connected digital twins present and develop core use cases to inform innovation strategy

outputs.title: Key milestones defined for achieving an ecosystem of digital twins in transport
activities: Identify key milestone activities and establish the timelines to achieve them, conduct regular reviews and update these

outputs.title: Identified infrastructure and services supporting requirements of connecting digital twins
activities: Identify who will provide and maintain those assets and services and establish relevant collaborations
Identify underlying infrastructure and services that will support the development and use of digital twins

outcomes: Strategic innovation plan to ensure a common path of travel for all stakeholders and delivery partners working within the connected digital twin environment
Clear understanding in the transport sector of how connected digital twins can provide value with recognised and repeatable value propositions

relevant_links.title: Digital Twins: Ethics and the Gemini Principles​
The Future of the Automotive Industry through Digitalisation​
Leader for research in digital twinning for decarbonising transport – UKRI
Develop a UK digital twinning research community with a NetworkPlus

outputs.title: Accessible procurement frameworks for connected digital twins
Appropriate routes for the investment and finance community to invest in connected digital twins and provide maintenance and management funding throughout the lifecycle
Commercial models in place to address funding needs for connected digital twins including maintenance
Marketing strategy in place to communicate achievements in digital twins and attract stakeholders
Baseline for digitised assets to establish the direction for digital twins in the transport sector
Use cases identified based on customer/industry/government needs and the value gained from combined data
Business case for use cases that require government funding and for those that industry will invest in
Operationalised connected digital twin use cases
Cyber-physical standards for transport sector infrastructure for better connection and communication
Cyber-physical infrastructure deployment and management framework
Legal framework for data access, sharing and authorisation, IP and certification requirements
Operational guidance on legal frameworks
An up-to-date systems map of the transport networks highlighting flow of information and interdependencies
Resilience and risk management framework
A well communicated vision outlining the purpose, benefits and strategic case for developing an ecosystem of connected digital twins in transport
Clear guidelines based on Gemini Principles detailing the values and ethical requirements for the delivery of a digital twin programme
Digital twin related programmes that focus on strategic priorities for transport have investment backing from senior leadership
Organisational alignment of relevant institutions in the ecosystem of connected digital twins with agreed roles and responsibilities
Scope of works and relevant key performance indicators including ESG metrics are in place to measure the success and objectives of the connected digital twins programme
Benefit realisation framework and metrics produced to track benefits to transport stakeholders, end customers and the environment
Agreed governance framework to ensure involvement from government, industry, academia and research and innovation funding organisations
Agreed communication and engagement plans
Materials including frameworks and artifacts to facilitate collaboration and co-creation of resources
Defined collaboration strategy to facilitate coordination between researchers, industry and the research and innovation funding community
Skills and capabilities gap analysed and barriers to closing skills gap identified
Skills and Competency framework developed for technical and non-technical skills required to develop a national capability in digital twins
Clear education pathways and capability enhancement programme, informed by gap analysis
Established links to academic centres of excellence and learning hubs to deliver the training required by the workforce
Clear guidance and interoperability standards for better information integration across transport digital twins
Toolkit to inform data collection, calibration and validation techniques
Appropriate governance frameworks to enable management, access and sharing of data
Data framework to coordinate different systems based on data requirements and data sharing protocols
Testing environment set up for digital twin components and processes
Physical infrastructure components in place (sensors, IoT devices, connectivity) that are secure by design
Digital infrastructure components in place (storage, high performance compute) that are secure by design
Detailed plans to address barriers and to provide high connectivity within cyber physical infrastructure
Framework for monitoring and managing of physical and digital assets
Forward-looking trust framework for data sharing in place
Embedded security-mindedness approach in industry, supply chains delivering digital twins
Secure infrastructure, data and data transfers
